candidate_problems.text: What precise architectural conditions are necessary and sufficient for a system to genuinely examine and revise its own goals, rather than merely manipulate internal proxies or meta-parameters while its effective objective remains unchanged? The conjecture posits an inspectable intermediate layer, but leaves open how to distinguish real goal-criticizability from superficial self-modeling or policy-level adaptation.

How can a revisable goal layer remain stable enough for coherent action while still being open to criticism, without collapsing into oscillation, self-undermining, or adversarial self-redefinition? The conjecture highlights bidirectional coupling between competence and evaluation, but the control problem of coupling strength, update rules, and invariants is unresolved.

Is weak integration between optimization and evaluative commitment actually the main driver of AI risk, compared with alternative explanations such as mesa-optimization, distribution shift, deceptive alignment, or power-seeking dynamics? The conjecture offers a structural diagnosis, but it remains an open empirical and theoretical question whether this layer-separation framework has greater explanatory and predictive power than competing safety models.
